1→/*
   2→ * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
   3→ */
   4→@file:Suppress("DEPRECATION_ERROR")
   5→
   6→package kotlinx.coroutines
   7→
   8→import kotlinx.atomicfu.*
   9→import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
  10→import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
  11→import kotlin.coroutines.*
  12→import kotlin.coroutines.intrinsics.*
  13→import kotlin.js.*
  14→import kotlin.jvm.*
  15→
  16→/**
  17→ * A concrete implementation of [Job]. It is optionally a child to a parent job.
  18→ *
  19→ * This is an open class designed for extension by more specific classes that might augment the
  20→ * state and mare store addition state information for completed jobs, like their result values.
  21→ *
  22→ * @param active when `true` the job is created in _active_ state, when `false` in _new_ state. See [Job] for details.
  23→ * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
  24→ */
  25→@Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR, message = "This is internal API and may be removed in the future releases")
  26→public open class JobSupport constructor(active: Boolean) : Job, ChildJob, ParentJob {
  27→    final override val key: CoroutineContext.Key<*> get() = Job
  28→
  29→    /*
  30→       === Internal states ===
  31→
  32→       name       state class              public state  description
  33→       ------     ------------             ------------  -----------
  34→       EMPTY_N    EmptyNew               : New           no listeners
  35→       EMPTY_A    EmptyActive            : Active        no listeners
  36→       SINGLE     JobNode                : Active        a single listener
  37→       SINGLE+    JobNode                : Active        a single listener + NodeList added as its next
  38→       LIST_N     InactiveNodeList       : New           a list of listeners (promoted once, does not got back to EmptyNew)
  39→       LIST_A     NodeList               : Active        a list of listeners (promoted once, does not got back to JobNode/EmptyActive)
  40→       COMPLETING Finishing              : Completing    has a list of listeners (promoted once from LIST_*)
  41→       CANCELLING Finishing              : Cancelling    -- " --
  42→       FINAL_C    Cancelled              : Cancelled     Cancelled (final state)
  43→       FINAL_R    <any>                  : Completed     produced some result
  44→
  45→       === Transitions ===
  46→
  47→           New states      Active states       Inactive states
  48→
  49→          +---------+       +---------+                          }
  50→          | EMPTY_N | ----> | EMPTY_A | ----+                    } Empty states
  51→          +---------+       +---------+     |                    }
  52→               |  |           |     ^       |    +----------+
  53→               |  |           |     |       +--> |  FINAL_* |
  54→               |  |           V     |       |    +----------+
  55→               |  |         +---------+     |                    }
  56→               |  |         | SINGLE  | ----+                    } JobNode states
  57→               |  |         +---------+     |                    }
  58→               |  |              |          |                    }
  59→               |  |              V          |                    }
  60→               |  |         +---------+     |                    }
  61→               |  +-------> | SINGLE+ | ----+                    }
  62→               |            +---------+     |                    }
  63→               |                 |          |
  64→               V                 V          |
  65→          +---------+       +---------+     |                    }
  66→          | LIST_N  | ----> | LIST_A  | ----+                    } [Inactive]NodeList states
  67→          +---------+       +---------+     |                    }
  68→             |   |             |   |        |
  69→             |   |    +--------+   |        |
  70→             |   |    |            V        |
  71→             |   |    |    +------------+   |   +------------+   }
  72→             |   +-------> | COMPLETING | --+-- | CANCELLING |   } Finishing states
  73→             |        |    +------------+       +------------+   }
  74→             |        |         |                    ^
  75→             |        |         |                    |
  76→             +--------+---------+--------------------+
  77→
  78→
  79→       This state machine and its transition matrix are optimized for the common case when a job is created in active
  80→       state (EMPTY_A), at most one completion listener is added to it during its life-time, and it completes
  81→       successfully without children (in this case it directly goes from EMPTY_A or SINGLE state to FINAL_R
  82→       state without going to COMPLETING state)
  83→
  84→       Note that the actual `_state` variable can also be a reference to atomic operation descriptor `OpDescriptor`
  85→
  86→       ---------- TIMELINE of state changes and notification in Job lifecycle ----------
  87→
  88→       | The longest possible chain of events in shown, shorter versions cut-through intermediate states,
  89→       |  while still performing all the notifications in this order.
  90→
  91→         + Job object is created
  92→       ## NEW: state == EMPTY_NEW | is InactiveNodeList
  93→         + initParentJob / initParentJobInternal (invokes attachChild on its parent, initializes parentHandle)
  94→         ~ waits for start
  95→         >> start / join / await invoked
  96→       ## ACTIVE: state == EMPTY_ACTIVE | is JobNode | is NodeList
  97→         + onStart (lazy coroutine is started)
  98→         ~ active coroutine is working (or scheduled to execution)
  99→         >> childCancelled / cancelImpl invoked
 100→       ## CANCELLING: state is Finishing, state.rootCause != null
 101→        ------ cancelling listeners are not admitted anymore, invokeOnCompletion(onCancelling=true) returns NonDisposableHandle
 102→        ------ new children get immediately cancelled, but are still admitted to the list
 103→         + onCancelling
 104→         + notifyCancelling (invoke all cancelling listeners -- cancel all children, suspended functions resume with exception)
 105→         + cancelParent (rootCause of cancellation is communicated to the parent, parent is cancelled, too)
 106→         ~ waits for completion of coroutine body
 107→         >> makeCompleting / makeCompletingOnce invoked
 108→       ## COMPLETING: state is Finishing, state.isCompleting == true
 109→        ------ new children are not admitted anymore, attachChild returns NonDisposableHandle
 110→         ~ waits for children
 111→         >> last child completes
 112→         - computes the final exception
 113→       ## SEALED: state is Finishing, state.isSealed == true
 114→        ------ cancel/childCancelled returns false (cannot handle exceptions anymore)
 115→         + cancelParent (final exception is communicated to the parent, parent incorporates it)
 116→         + handleJobException ("launch" StandaloneCoroutine invokes CoroutineExceptionHandler)
 117→       ## COMPLETE: state !is Incomplete (CompletedExceptionally | Cancelled)
 118→        ------ completion listeners are not admitted anymore, invokeOnCompletion returns NonDisposableHandle
 119→         + parentHandle.dispose
 120→         + notifyCompletion (invoke all completion listeners)
 121→         + onCompletionInternal / onCompleted / onCancelled
 122→
 123→       ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------
 124→     */
 125→
 126→    // Note: use shared objects while we have no listeners
 127→    private val _state = atomic<Any?>(if (active) EMPTY_ACTIVE else EMPTY_NEW)
 128→
 129→    private val _parentHandle = atomic<ChildHandle?>(null)
 130→    internal var parentHandle: ChildHandle?
 131→        get() = _parentHandle.value
 132→        set(value) { _parentHandle.value = value }
 133→
 134→    override val parent: Job?
 135→        get() = parentHandle?.parent
 136→
 137→    // ------------ initialization ------------
 138→
 139→    /**
 140→     * Initializes parent job.
 141→     * It shall be invoked at most once after construction after all other initialization.
 142→     */
 143→    protected fun initParentJob(parent: Job?) {
 144→        assert { parentHandle == null }
 145→        if (parent == null) {
 146→            parentHandle = NonDisposableHandle
 147→            return
 148→        }
 149→        parent.start() // make sure the parent is started
 150→        @Suppress("DEPRECATION")
 151→        val handle = parent.attachChild(this)
 152→        parentHandle = handle
 153→        // now check our state _after_ registering (see tryFinalizeSimpleState order of actions)
 154→        if (isCompleted) {
 155→            handle.dispose()
 156→            parentHandle = NonDisposableHandle // release it just in case, to aid GC
 157→        }
 158→    }
 159→
 160→    // ------------ state query ------------
 161→    /**
 162→     * Returns current state of this job.
 163→     * If final state of the job is [Incomplete], then it is boxed into [IncompleteStateBox]
 164→     * and should be [unboxed][unboxState] before returning to user code.
 165→     */
 166→    internal val state: Any? get() {
 167→        _state.loop { state -> // helper loop on state (complete in-progress atomic operations)
 168→            if (state !is OpDescriptor) return state
 169→            state.perform(this)
 170→        }
 171→    }
 172→
 173→    /**
 174→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
 175→     */
 176→    private inline fun loopOnState(block: (Any?) -> Unit): Nothing {
 177→        while (true) {
 178→            block(state)
 179→        }
 180→    }
 181→
 182→    public override val isActive: Boolean get() {
 183→        val state = this.state
 184→        return state is Incomplete && state.isActive
 185→    }
 186→
 187→    public final override val isCompleted: Boolean get() = state !is Incomplete
 188→
 189→    public final override val isCancelled: Boolean get() {
 190→        val state = this.state
 191→        return state is CompletedExceptionally || (state is Finishing && state.isCancelling)
 192→    }
 193→
 194→    // ------------ state update ------------
 195→
 196→    // Finalizes Finishing -> Completed (terminal state) transition.
 197→    // ## IMPORTANT INVARIANT: Only one thread can be concurrently invoking this method.
 198→    // Returns final state that was created and updated to
 199→    private fun finalizeFinishingState(state: Finishing, proposedUpdate: Any?): Any? {
 200→        /*
 201→         * Note: proposed state can be Incomplete, e.g.
 202→         * async {
 203→         *     something.invokeOnCompletion {} // <- returns handle which implements Incomplete under the hood
 204→         * }
 205→         */
 206→        assert { this.state === state } // consistency check -- it cannot change
 207→        assert { !state.isSealed } // consistency check -- cannot be sealed yet
 208→        assert { state.isCompleting } // consistency check -- must be marked as completing
 209→        val proposedException = (proposedUpdate as? CompletedExceptionally)?.cause
 210→        // Create the final exception and seal the state so that no more exceptions can be added
 211→        var wasCancelling = false // KLUDGE: we cannot have contract for our own expect fun synchronized
 212→        val finalException = synchronized(state) {
 213→            wasCancelling = state.isCancelling
 214→            val exceptions = state.sealLocked(proposedException)
 215→            val finalCause = getFinalRootCause(state, exceptions)
 216→            if (finalCause != null) addSuppressedExceptions(finalCause, exceptions)
 217→            finalCause
 218→        }
 219→        // Create the final state object
 220→        val finalState = when {
 221→            // was not cancelled (no exception) -> use proposed update value
 222→            finalException == null -> proposedUpdate
 223→            // small optimization when we can used proposeUpdate object as is on cancellation
 224→            finalException === proposedException -> proposedUpdate
 225→            // cancelled job final state
 226→            else -> CompletedExceptionally(finalException)
 227→        }
 228→        // Now handle the final exception
 229→        if (finalException != null) {
 230→            val handled = cancelParent(finalException) || handleJobException(finalException)
 231→            if (handled) (finalState as CompletedExceptionally).makeHandled()
 232→        }
 233→        // Process state updates for the final state before the state of the Job is actually set to the final state
 234→        // to avoid races where outside observer may see the job in the final state, yet exception is not handled yet.
 235→        if (!wasCancelling) onCancelling(finalException)
 236→        onCompletionInternal(finalState)
 237→        // Then CAS to completed state -> it must succeed
 238→        val casSuccess = _state.compareAndSet(state, finalState.boxIncomplete())
 239→        assert { casSuccess }
 240→        // And process all post-completion actions
 241→        completeStateFinalization(state, finalState)
 242→        return finalState
 243→    }
 244→
 245→    private fun getFinalRootCause(state: Finishing, exceptions: List<Throwable>): Throwable? {
 246→        // A case of no exceptions
 247→        if (exceptions.isEmpty()) {
 248→            // materialize cancellation exception if it was not materialized yet
 249→            if (state.isCancelling) return defaultCancellationException()
 250→            return null
 251→        }
 252→        /*
 253→         * 1) If we have non-CE, use it as root cause
 254→         * 2) If our original cause was TCE, use *non-original* TCE because of the special nature of TCE
 255→         *    * It is a CE, so it's not reported by children
 256→         *    * The first instance (cancellation cause) is created by timeout coroutine and has no meaningful stacktrace
 257→         *    * The potential second instance is thrown by withTimeout lexical block itself, then it has recovered stacktrace
 258→         * 3) Just return the very first CE
 259→         */
 260→        val firstNonCancellation = exceptions.firstOrNull { it !is CancellationException }
 261→        if (firstNonCancellation != null) return firstNonCancellation
 262→        val first = exceptions[0]
 263→        if (first is TimeoutCancellationException) {
 264→            val detailedTimeoutException = exceptions.firstOrNull { it !== first && it is TimeoutCancellationException }
 265→            if (detailedTimeoutException != null) return detailedTimeoutException
 266→        }
 267→        return first
 268→    }
 269→
 270→    private fun addSuppressedExceptions(rootCause: Throwable, exceptions: List<Throwable>) {
 271→        if (exceptions.size <= 1) return // nothing more to do here
 272→        val seenExceptions = identitySet<Throwable>(exceptions.size)
 273→        /*
 274→         * Note that root cause may be a recovered exception as well.
 275→         * To avoid cycles we unwrap the root cause and check for self-suppression against unwrapped cause,
 276→         * but add suppressed exceptions to the recovered root cause (as it is our final exception)
 277→         */
 278→        val unwrappedCause = unwrap(rootCause)
 279→        for (exception in exceptions) {
 280→            val unwrapped = unwrap(exception)
 281→            if (unwrapped !== rootCause && unwrapped !== unwrappedCause &&
 282→                unwrapped !is CancellationException && seenExceptions.add(unwrapped)) {
 283→                rootCause.addSuppressedThrowable(unwrapped)
 284→            }
 285→        }
 286→    }
 287→
 288→    // fast-path method to finalize normally completed coroutines without children
 289→    // returns true if complete, and afterCompletion(update) shall be called
 290→    private fun tryFinalizeSimpleState(state: Incomplete, update: Any?): Boolean {
 291→        assert { state is Empty || state is JobNode } // only simple state without lists where children can concurrently add
 292→        assert { update !is CompletedExceptionally } // only for normal completion
 293→        if (!_state.compareAndSet(state, update.boxIncomplete())) return false
 294→        onCancelling(null) // simple state is not a failure
 295→        onCompletionInternal(update)
 296→        completeStateFinalization(state, update)
 297→        return true
 298→    }
 299→
 300→    // suppressed == true when any exceptions were suppressed while building the final completion cause
 301→    private fun completeStateFinalization(state: Incomplete, update: Any?) {
 302→        /*
 303→         * Now the job in THE FINAL state. We need to properly handle the resulting state.
 304→         * Order of various invocations here is important.
 305→         *
 306→         * 1) Unregister from parent job.
 307→         */
 308→        parentHandle?.let {
 309→            it.dispose() // volatile read parentHandle _after_ state was updated
 310→            parentHandle = NonDisposableHandle // release it just in case, to aid GC
 311→        }
 312→        val cause = (update as? CompletedExceptionally)?.cause
 313→        /*
 314→         * 2) Invoke completion handlers: .join(), callbacks etc.
 315→         *    It's important to invoke them only AFTER exception handling and everything else, see #208
 316→         */
 317→        if (state is JobNode) { // SINGLE/SINGLE+ state -- one completion handler (common case)
 318→            try {
 319→                state.invoke(cause)
 320→            } catch (ex: Throwable) {
 321→                handleOnCompletionException(CompletionHandlerException("Exception in completion handler $state for $this", ex))
 322→            }
 323→        } else {
 324→            state.list?.notifyCompletion(cause)
 325→        }
 326→    }
 327→
 328→    private fun notifyCancelling(list: NodeList, cause: Throwable) {
 329→        // first cancel our own children
 330→        onCancelling(cause)
 331→        notifyHandlers<JobCancellingNode>(list, cause)
 332→        // then cancel parent
 333→        cancelParent(cause) // tentative cancellation -- does not matter if there is no parent
 334→    }
 335→
 336→    /**
 337→     * The method that is invoked when the job is cancelled to possibly propagate cancellation to the parent.
 338→     * Returns `true` if the parent is responsible for handling the exception, `false` otherwise.
 339→     *
 340→     * Invariant: never returns `false` for instances of [CancellationException], otherwise such exception
 341→     * may leak to the [CoroutineExceptionHandler].
 342→     */
 343→    private fun cancelParent(cause: Throwable): Boolean {
 344→        // Is scoped coroutine -- don't propagate, will be rethrown
 345→        if (isScopedCoroutine) return true
 346→
 347→        /* CancellationException is considered "normal" and parent usually is not cancelled when child produces it.
 348→         * This allow parent to cancel its children (normally) without being cancelled itself, unless
 349→         * child crashes and produce some other exception during its completion.
 350→         */
 351→        val isCancellation = cause is CancellationException
 352→        val parent = parentHandle
 353→        // No parent -- ignore CE, report other exceptions.
 354→        if (parent === null || parent === NonDisposableHandle) {
 355→            return isCancellation
 356→        }
 357→
 358→        // Notify parent but don't forget to check cancellation
 359→        return parent.childCancelled(cause) || isCancellation
 360→    }
 361→
 362→    private fun NodeList.notifyCompletion(cause: Throwable?) =
 363→        notifyHandlers<JobNode>(this, cause)
 364→
 365→    private inline fun <reified T: JobNode> notifyHandlers(list: NodeList, cause: Throwable?) {
 366→        var exception: Throwable? = null
 367→        list.forEach<T> { node ->
 368→            try {
 369→                node.invoke(cause)
 370→            } catch (ex: Throwable) {
 371→                exception?.apply { addSuppressedThrowable(ex) } ?: run {
 372→                    exception =  CompletionHandlerException("Exception in completion handler $node for $this", ex)
 373→                }
 374→            }
 375→        }
 376→        exception?.let { handleOnCompletionException(it) }
 377→    }
 378→
 379→    public final override fun start(): Boolean {
 380→        loopOnState { state ->
 381→            when (startInternal(state)) {
 382→                FALSE -> return false
 383→                TRUE -> return true
 384→            }
 385→        }
 386→    }
 387→
 388→    // returns: RETRY/FALSE/TRUE:
 389→    //   FALSE when not new,
 390→    //   TRUE  when started
 391→    //   RETRY when need to retry
 392→    private fun startInternal(state: Any?): Int {
 393→        when (state) {
 394→            is Empty -> { // EMPTY_X state -- no completion handlers
 395→                if (state.isActive) return FALSE // already active
 396→                if (!_state.compareAndSet(state, EMPTY_ACTIVE)) return RETRY
 397→                onStart()
 398→                return TRUE
 399→            }
 400→            is InactiveNodeList -> { // LIST state -- inactive with a list of completion handlers
 401→                if (!_state.compareAndSet(state, state.list)) return RETRY
 402→                onStart()
 403→                return TRUE
 404→            }
 405→            else -> return FALSE // not a new state
 406→        }
 407→    }
 408→
 409→    /**
 410→     * Override to provide the actual [start] action.
 411→     * This function is invoked exactly once when non-active coroutine is [started][start].
 412→     */
 413→    protected open fun onStart() {}
 414→
 415→    public final override fun getCancellationException(): CancellationException =
 416→        when (val state = this.state) {
 417→            is Finishing -> state.rootCause?.toCancellationException("$classSimpleName is cancelling")
 418→                ?: error("Job is still new or active: $this")
 419→            is Incomplete -> error("Job is still new or active: $this")
 420→            is CompletedExceptionally -> state.cause.toCancellationException()
 421→            else -> JobCancellationException("$classSimpleName has completed normally", null, this)
 422→        }
 423→
 424→    protected fun Throwable.toCancellationException(message: String? = null): CancellationException =
 425→        this as? CancellationException ?: defaultCancellationException(message, this)
 426→
 427→    /**
 428→     * Returns the cause that signals the completion of this job -- it returns the original
 429→     * [cancel] cause, [CancellationException] or **`null` if this job had completed normally**.
 430→     * This function throws [IllegalStateException] when invoked for an job that has not [completed][isCompleted] nor
 431→     * is being cancelled yet.
 432→     */
 433→    protected val completionCause: Throwable?
 434→        get() = when (val state = state) {
 435→            is Finishing -> state.rootCause
 436→                ?: error("Job is still new or active: $this")
 437→            is Incomplete -> error("Job is still new or active: $this")
 438→            is CompletedExceptionally -> state.cause
 439→            else -> null
 440→        }
 441→
 442→    /**
 443→     * Returns `true` when [completionCause] exception was handled by parent coroutine.
 444→     */
 445→    protected val completionCauseHandled: Boolean
 446→        get() = state.let { it is CompletedExceptionally && it.handled }
 447→
 448→    @Suppress("OverridingDeprecatedMember")
 449→    public final override fun invokeOnCompletion(handler: CompletionHandler): DisposableHandle =
 450→        invokeOnCompletion(onCancelling = false, invokeImmediately = true, handler = handler)
 451→
 452→    public final override fun invokeOnCompletion(
 453→        onCancelling: Boolean,
 454→        invokeImmediately: Boolean,
 455→        handler: CompletionHandler
 456→    ): DisposableHandle {
 457→        // Create node upfront -- for common cases it just initializes JobNode.job field,
 458→        // for user-defined handlers it allocates a JobNode object that we might not need, but this is Ok.
 459→        val node: JobNode = makeNode(handler, onCancelling)
 460→        loopOnState { state ->
 461→            when (state) {
 462→                is Empty -> { // EMPTY_X state -- no completion handlers
 463→                    if (state.isActive) {
 464→                        // try move to SINGLE state
 465→                        if (_state.compareAndSet(state, node)) return node
 466→                    } else
 467→                        promoteEmptyToNodeList(state) // that way we can add listener for non-active coroutine
 468→                }
 469→                is Incomplete -> {
 470→                    val list = state.list
 471→                    if (list == null) { // SINGLE/SINGLE+
 472→                        promoteSingleToNodeList(state as JobNode)
 473→                    } else {
 474→                        var rootCause: Throwable? = null
 475→                        var handle: DisposableHandle = NonDisposableHandle
 476→                        if (onCancelling && state is Finishing) {
 477→                            synchronized(state) {
 478→                                // check if we are installing cancellation handler on job that is being cancelled
 479→                                rootCause = state.rootCause // != null if cancelling job
 480→                                // We add node to the list in two cases --- either the job is not being cancelled
 481→                                // or we are adding a child to a coroutine that is not completing yet
 482→                                if (rootCause == null || handler.isHandlerOf<ChildHandleNode>() && !state.isCompleting) {
 483→                                    // Note: add node the list while holding lock on state (make sure it cannot change)
 484→                                    if (!addLastAtomic(state, list, node)) return@loopOnState // retry
 485→                                    // just return node if we don't have to invoke handler (not cancelling yet)
 486→                                    if (rootCause == null) return node
 487→                                    // otherwise handler is invoked immediately out of the synchronized section & handle returned
 488→                                    handle = node
 489→                                }
 490→                            }
 491→                        }
 492→                        if (rootCause != null) {
 493→                            // Note: attachChild uses invokeImmediately, so it gets invoked when adding to cancelled job
 494→                            if (invokeImmediately) handler.invokeIt(rootCause)
 495→                            return handle
 496→                        } else {
 497→                            if (addLastAtomic(state, list, node)) return node
 498→                        }
 499→                    }
 500→                }
 501→                else -> { // is complete
 502→                    // :KLUDGE: We have to invoke a handler in platform-specific way via `invokeIt` extension,
 503→                    // because we play type tricks on Kotlin/JS and handler is not necessarily a function there
 504→                    if (invokeImmediately) handler.invokeIt((state as? CompletedExceptionally)?.cause)
 505→                    return NonDisposableHandle
 506→                }
 507→            }
 508→        }
 509→    }
 510→
 511→    private fun makeNode(handler: CompletionHandler, onCancelling: Boolean): JobNode {
 512→        val node = if (onCancelling) {
 513→            (handler as? JobCancellingNode)
 514→                ?: InvokeOnCancelling(handler)
 515→        } else {
 516→            (handler as? JobNode)
 517→                ?.also { assert { it !is JobCancellingNode } }
 518→                ?: InvokeOnCompletion(handler)
 519→        }
 520→        node.job = this
 521→        return node
 522→    }
 523→
 524→    private fun addLastAtomic(expect: Any, list: NodeList, node: JobNode) =
 525→        list.addLastIf(node) { this.state === expect }
 526→
 527→    private fun promoteEmptyToNodeList(state: Empty) {
 528→        // try to promote it to LIST state with the corresponding state
 529→        val list = NodeList()
 530→        val update = if (state.isActive) list else InactiveNodeList(list)
 531→        _state.compareAndSet(state, update)
 532→    }
 533→
 534→    private fun promoteSingleToNodeList(state: JobNode) {
 535→        // try to promote it to list (SINGLE+ state)
 536→        state.addOneIfEmpty(NodeList())
 537→        // it must be in SINGLE+ state or state has changed (node could have need removed from state)
 538→        val list = state.nextNode // either our NodeList or somebody else won the race, updated state
 539→        // just attempt converting it to list if state is still the same, then we'll continue lock-free loop
 540→        _state.compareAndSet(state, list)
 541→    }
 542→
 543→    public final override suspend fun join() {
 544→        if (!joinInternal()) { // fast-path no wait
 545→            coroutineContext.ensureActive()
 546→            return // do not suspend
 547→        }
 548→        return joinSuspend() // slow-path wait
 549→    }
 550→
 551→    private fun joinInternal(): Boolean {
 552→        loopOnState { state ->
 553→            if (state !is Incomplete) return false // not active anymore (complete) -- no need to wait
 554→            if (startInternal(state) >= 0) return true // wait unless need to retry
 555→        }
 556→    }
 557→
 558→    private suspend fun joinSuspend() = suspendCancellableCoroutine<Unit> { cont ->
 559→        // We have to invoke join() handler only on cancellation, on completion we will be resumed regularly without handlers
 560→        cont.disposeOnCancellation(invokeOnCompletion(handler = ResumeOnCompletion(cont).asHandler))
 561→    }
 562→
 563→    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 564→    public final override val onJoin: SelectClause0
 565→        get() = SelectClause0Impl(
 566→            clauseObject = this@JobSupport,
 567→            regFunc = JobSupport::registerSelectForOnJoin as RegistrationFunction
 568→        )
 569→
 570→    @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")
 571→    private fun registerSelectForOnJoin(select: SelectInstance<*>, ignoredParam: Any?) {
 572→        if (!joinInternal()) {
 573→            select.selectInRegistrationPhase(Unit)
 574→            return
 575→        }
 576→        val disposableHandle = invokeOnCompletion(SelectOnJoinCompletionHandler(select).asHandler)
 577→        select.disposeOnCompletion(disposableHandle)
 578→    }
 579→
 580→    private inner class SelectOnJoinCompletionHandler(
 581→        private val select: SelectInstance<*>
 582→    ) : JobNode() {
 583→        override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) {
 584→            select.trySelect(this@JobSupport, Unit)
 585→        }
 586→    }
 587→
 588→    /**
 589→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
 590→     */
 591→    internal fun removeNode(node: JobNode) {
 592→        // remove logic depends on the state of the job
 593→        loopOnState { state ->
 594→            when (state) {
 595→                is JobNode -> { // SINGE/SINGLE+ state -- one completion handler
 596→                    if (state !== node) return // a different job node --> we were already removed
 597→                    // try remove and revert back to empty state
 598→                    if (_state.compareAndSet(state, EMPTY_ACTIVE)) return
 599→                }
 600→                is Incomplete -> { // may have a list of completion handlers
 601→                    // remove node from the list if there is a list
 602→                    if (state.list != null) node.remove()
 603→                    return
 604→                }
 605→                else -> return // it is complete and does not have any completion handlers
 606→            }
 607→        }
 608→    }
 609→
 610→    /**
 611→     * Returns `true` for job that do not have "body block" to complete and should immediately go into
 612→     * completing state and start waiting for children.
 613→     *
 614→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
 615→     */
 616→    internal open val onCancelComplete: Boolean get() = false
 617→
 618→    // external cancel with cause, never invoked implicitly from internal machinery
 619→    public override fun cancel(cause: CancellationException?) {
 620→        cancelInternal(cause ?: defaultCancellationException())
 621→    }
 622→
 623→    protected open fun cancellationExceptionMessage(): String = "Job was cancelled"
 624→
 625→    // HIDDEN in Job interface. Invoked only by legacy compiled code.
 626→    // external cancel with (optional) cause, never invoked implicitly from internal machinery
 627→    @Deprecated(level = DeprecationLevel.HIDDEN, message = "Added since 1.2.0 for binary compatibility with versions <= 1.1.x")
 628→    public override fun cancel(cause: Throwable?): Boolean {
 629→        cancelInternal(cause?.toCancellationException() ?: defaultCancellationException())
 630→        return true
 631→    }
 632→
 633→    // It is overridden in channel-linked implementation
 634→    public open fun cancelInternal(cause: Throwable) {
 635→        cancelImpl(cause)
 636→    }
 637→
 638→    // Parent is cancelling child
 639→    public final override fun parentCancelled(parentJob: ParentJob) {
 640→        cancelImpl(parentJob)
 641→    }
 642→
 643→    /**
 644→     * Child was cancelled with a cause.
 645→     * In this method parent decides whether it cancels itself (e.g. on a critical failure) and whether it handles the exception of the child.
 646→     * It is overridden in supervisor implementations to completely ignore any child cancellation.
 647→     * Returns `true` if exception is handled, `false` otherwise (then caller is responsible for handling an exception)
 648→     *
 649→     * Invariant: never returns `false` for instances of [CancellationException], otherwise such exception
 650→     * may leak to the [CoroutineExceptionHandler].
 651→     */
 652→    public open fun childCancelled(cause: Throwable): Boolean {
 653→        if (cause is CancellationException) return true
 654→        return cancelImpl(cause) && handlesException
 655→    }
 656→
 657→    /**
 658→     * Makes this [Job] cancelled with a specified [cause].
 659→     * It is used in [AbstractCoroutine]-derived classes when there is an internal failure.
 660→     */
 661→    public fun cancelCoroutine(cause: Throwable?): Boolean = cancelImpl(cause)
 662→
 663→    // cause is Throwable or ParentJob when cancelChild was invoked
 664→    // returns true is exception was handled, false otherwise
 665→    internal fun cancelImpl(cause: Any?): Boolean {
 666→        var finalState: Any? = COMPLETING_ALREADY
 667→        if (onCancelComplete) {
 668→            // make sure it is completing, if cancelMakeCompleting returns state it means it had make it
 669→            // completing and had recorded exception
 670→            finalState = cancelMakeCompleting(cause)
 671→            if (finalState === COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN) return true
 672→        }
 673→        if (finalState === COMPLETING_ALREADY) {
 674→            finalState = makeCancelling(cause)
 675→        }
 676→        return when {
 677→            finalState === COMPLETING_ALREADY -> true
 678→            finalState === COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN -> true
 679→            finalState === TOO_LATE_TO_CANCEL -> false
 680→            else -> {
 681→                afterCompletion(finalState)
 682→                true
 683→            }
 684→        }
 685→    }
 686→
 687→    // cause is Throwable or ParentJob when cancelChild was invoked
 688→    // It contains a loop and never returns COMPLETING_RETRY, can return
 689→    // COMPLETING_ALREADY -- if already completed/completing
 690→    // COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN -- if started waiting for children
 691→    // final state -- when completed, for call to afterCompletion
 692→    private fun cancelMakeCompleting(cause: Any?): Any? {
 693→        loopOnState { state ->
 694→            if (state !is Incomplete || state is Finishing && state.isCompleting) {
 695→                // already completed/completing, do not even create exception to propose update
 696→                return COMPLETING_ALREADY
 697→            }
 698→            val proposedUpdate = CompletedExceptionally(createCauseException(cause))
 699→            val finalState = tryMakeCompleting(state, proposedUpdate)
 700→            if (finalState !== COMPLETING_RETRY) return finalState
 701→        }
 702→    }
 703→
 704→    @Suppress("NOTHING_TO_INLINE") // Save a stack frame
 705→    internal inline fun defaultCancellationException(message: String? = null, cause: Throwable? = null) =
 706→        JobCancellationException(message ?: cancellationExceptionMessage(), cause, this)
 707→
 708→    override fun getChildJobCancellationCause(): CancellationException {
 709→        // determine root cancellation cause of this job (why is it cancelling its children?)
 710→        val state = this.state
 711→        val rootCause = when (state) {
 712→            is Finishing -> state.rootCause
 713→            is CompletedExceptionally -> state.cause
 714→            is Incomplete -> error("Cannot be cancelling child in this state: $state")
 715→            else -> null // create exception with the below code on normal completion
 716→        }
 717→        return (rootCause as? CancellationException) ?: JobCancellationException("Parent job is ${stateString(state)}", rootCause, this)
 718→    }
 719→
 720→    // cause is Throwable or ParentJob when cancelChild was invoked
 721→    private fun createCauseException(cause: Any?): Throwable = when (cause) {
 722→        is Throwable? -> cause ?: defaultCancellationException()
 723→        else -> (cause as ParentJob).getChildJobCancellationCause()
 724→    }
 725→
 726→    // transitions to Cancelling state
 727→    // cause is Throwable or ParentJob when cancelChild was invoked
 728→    // It contains a loop and never returns COMPLETING_RETRY, can return
 729→    // COMPLETING_ALREADY -- if already completing or successfully made cancelling, added exception
 730→    // COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN -- if started waiting for children, added exception
 731→    // TOO_LATE_TO_CANCEL -- too late to cancel, did not add exception
 732→    // final state -- when completed, for call to afterCompletion
 733→    private fun makeCancelling(cause: Any?): Any? {
 734→        var causeExceptionCache: Throwable? = null // lazily init result of createCauseException(cause)
 735→        loopOnState { state ->
 736→            when (state) {
 737→                is Finishing -> { // already finishing -- collect exceptions
 738→                    val notifyRootCause = synchronized(state) {
 739→                        if (state.isSealed) return TOO_LATE_TO_CANCEL // already sealed -- cannot add exception nor mark cancelled
 740→                        // add exception, do nothing is parent is cancelling child that is already being cancelled
 741→                        val wasCancelling = state.isCancelling // will notify if was not cancelling
 742→                        // Materialize missing exception if it is the first exception (otherwise -- don't)
 743→                        if (cause != null || !wasCancelling) {
 744→                            val causeException = causeExceptionCache ?: createCauseException(cause).also { causeExceptionCache = it }
 745→                            state.addExceptionLocked(causeException)
 746→                        }
 747→                        // take cause for notification if was not in cancelling state before
 748→                        state.rootCause.takeIf { !wasCancelling }
 749→                    }
 750→                    notifyRootCause?.let { notifyCancelling(state.list, it) }
 751→                    return COMPLETING_ALREADY
 752→                }
 753→                is Incomplete -> {
 754→                    // Not yet finishing -- try to make it cancelling
 755→                    val causeException = causeExceptionCache ?: createCauseException(cause).also { causeExceptionCache = it }
 756→                    if (state.isActive) {
 757→                        // active state becomes cancelling
 758→                        if (tryMakeCancelling(state, causeException)) return COMPLETING_ALREADY
 759→                    } else {
 760→                        // non active state starts completing
 761→                        val finalState = tryMakeCompleting(state, CompletedExceptionally(causeException))
 762→                        when {
 763→                            finalState === COMPLETING_ALREADY -> error("Cannot happen in $state")
 764→                            finalState === COMPLETING_RETRY -> return@loopOnState
 765→                            else -> return finalState
 766→                        }
 767→                    }
 768→                }
 769→                else -> return TOO_LATE_TO_CANCEL // already complete
 770→            }
 771→        }
 772→    }
 773→
 774→    // Performs promotion of incomplete coroutine state to NodeList for the purpose of
 775→    // converting coroutine state to Cancelling, returns null when need to retry
 776→    private fun getOrPromoteCancellingList(state: Incomplete): NodeList? = state.list ?:
 777→        when (state) {
 778→            is Empty -> NodeList() // we can allocate new empty list that'll get integrated into Cancelling state
 779→            is JobNode -> {
 780→                // SINGLE/SINGLE+ must be promoted to NodeList first, because otherwise we cannot
 781→                // correctly capture a reference to it
 782→                promoteSingleToNodeList(state)
 783→                null // retry
 784→            }
 785→            else -> error("State should have list: $state")
 786→        }
 787→
 788→    // try make new Cancelling state on the condition that we're still in the expected state
 789→    private fun tryMakeCancelling(state: Incomplete, rootCause: Throwable): Boolean {
 790→        assert { state !is Finishing } // only for non-finishing states
 791→        assert { state.isActive } // only for active states
 792→        // get state's list or else promote to list to correctly operate on child lists
 793→        val list = getOrPromoteCancellingList(state) ?: return false
 794→        // Create cancelling state (with rootCause!)
 795→        val cancelling = Finishing(list, false, rootCause)
 796→        if (!_state.compareAndSet(state, cancelling)) return false
 797→        // Notify listeners
 798→        notifyCancelling(list, rootCause)
 799→        return true
 800→    }
 801→
 802→    /**
 803→     * Completes this job. Used by [CompletableDeferred.complete] (and exceptionally)
 804→     * and by [JobImpl.cancel]. It returns `false` on repeated invocation
 805→     * (when this job is already completing).
 806→     */
 807→    internal fun makeCompleting(proposedUpdate: Any?): Boolean {
 808→        loopOnState { state ->
 809→            val finalState = tryMakeCompleting(state, proposedUpdate)
 810→            when {
 811→                finalState === COMPLETING_ALREADY -> return false
 812→                finalState === COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN -> return true
 813→                finalState === COMPLETING_RETRY -> return@loopOnState
 814→                else -> {
 815→                    afterCompletion(finalState)
 816→                    return true
 817→                }
 818→            }
 819→        }
 820→    } 
 821→
 822→    /**
 823→     * Completes this job. Used by [AbstractCoroutine.resume].
 824→     * It throws [IllegalStateException] on repeated invocation (when this job is already completing).
 825→     * Returns:
 826→     * * [COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN] if started waiting for children.
 827→     * * Final state otherwise (caller should do [afterCompletion])
 828→     */
 829→    internal fun makeCompletingOnce(proposedUpdate: Any?): Any? {
 830→        loopOnState { state ->
 831→            val finalState = tryMakeCompleting(state, proposedUpdate)
 832→            when {
 833→                finalState === COMPLETING_ALREADY ->
 834→                    throw IllegalStateException(
 835→                        "Job $this is already complete or completing, " +
 836→                            "but is being completed with $proposedUpdate", proposedUpdate.exceptionOrNull
 837→                    )
 838→                finalState === COMPLETING_RETRY -> return@loopOnState
 839→                else -> return finalState // COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN or final state
 840→            }
 841→        }
 842→    }
 843→
 844→    // Returns one of COMPLETING symbols or final state:
 845→    // COMPLETING_ALREADY -- when already complete or completing
 846→    // COMPLETING_RETRY -- when need to retry due to interference
 847→    // COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN -- when made completing and is waiting for children
 848→    // final state -- when completed, for call to afterCompletion
 849→    private fun tryMakeCompleting(state: Any?, proposedUpdate: Any?): Any? {
 850→        if (state !is Incomplete)
 851→            return COMPLETING_ALREADY
 852→        /*
 853→         * FAST PATH -- no children to wait for && simple state (no list) && not cancelling => can complete immediately
 854→         * Cancellation (failures) always have to go through Finishing state to serialize exception handling.
 855→         * Otherwise, there can be a race between (completed state -> handled exception and newly attached child/join)
 856→         * which may miss unhandled exception.
 857→         */
 858→        if ((state is Empty || state is JobNode) && state !is ChildHandleNode && proposedUpdate !is CompletedExceptionally) {
 859→            if (tryFinalizeSimpleState(state, proposedUpdate)) {
 860→                // Completed successfully on fast path -- return updated state
 861→                return proposedUpdate
 862→            }
 863→            return COMPLETING_RETRY
 864→        }
 865→        // The separate slow-path function to simplify profiling
 866→        return tryMakeCompletingSlowPath(state, proposedUpdate)
 867→    }
 868→
 869→    // Returns one of COMPLETING symbols or final state:
 870→    // COMPLETING_ALREADY -- when already complete or completing
 871→    // COMPLETING_RETRY -- when need to retry due to interference
 872→    // COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN -- when made completing and is waiting for children
 873→    // final state -- when completed, for call to afterCompletion
 874→    private fun tryMakeCompletingSlowPath(state: Incomplete, proposedUpdate: Any?): Any? {
 875→        // get state's list or else promote to list to correctly operate on child lists
 876→        val list = getOrPromoteCancellingList(state) ?: return COMPLETING_RETRY
 877→        // promote to Finishing state if we are not in it yet
 878→        // This promotion has to be atomic w.r.t to state change, so that a coroutine that is not active yet
 879→        // atomically transition to finishing & completing state
 880→        val finishing = state as? Finishing ?: Finishing(list, false, null)
 881→        // must synchronize updates to finishing state
 882→        var notifyRootCause: Throwable? = null
 883→        synchronized(finishing) {
 884→            // check if this state is already completing
 885→            if (finishing.isCompleting) return COMPLETING_ALREADY
 886→            // mark as completing
 887→            finishing.isCompleting = true
 888→            // if we need to promote to finishing then atomically do it here.
 889→            // We do it as early is possible while still holding the lock. This ensures that we cancelImpl asap
 890→            // (if somebody else is faster) and we synchronize all the threads on this finishing lock asap.
 891→            if (finishing !== state) {
 892→                if (!_state.compareAndSet(state, finishing)) return COMPLETING_RETRY
 893→            }
 894→            // ## IMPORTANT INVARIANT: Only one thread (that had set isCompleting) can go past this point
 895→            assert { !finishing.isSealed } // cannot be sealed
 896→            // add new proposed exception to the finishing state
 897→            val wasCancelling = finishing.isCancelling
 898→            (proposedUpdate as? CompletedExceptionally)?.let { finishing.addExceptionLocked(it.cause) }
 899→            // If it just becomes cancelling --> must process cancelling notifications
 900→            notifyRootCause = finishing.rootCause.takeIf { !wasCancelling }
 901→        }
 902→        // process cancelling notification here -- it cancels all the children _before_ we start to to wait them (sic!!!)
 903→        notifyRootCause?.let { notifyCancelling(list, it) }
 904→        // now wait for children
 905→        val child = firstChild(state)
 906→        if (child != null && tryWaitForChild(finishing, child, proposedUpdate))
 907→            return COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN
 908→        // otherwise -- we have not children left (all were already cancelled?)
 909→        return finalizeFinishingState(finishing, proposedUpdate)
 910→    }
 911→
 912→    private val Any?.exceptionOrNull: Throwable?
 913→        get() = (this as? CompletedExceptionally)?.cause
 914→
 915→    private fun firstChild(state: Incomplete) =
 916→        state as? ChildHandleNode ?: state.list?.nextChild()
 917→
 918→    // return false when there is no more incomplete children to wait
 919→    // ## IMPORTANT INVARIANT: Only one thread can be concurrently invoking this method.
 920→    private tailrec fun tryWaitForChild(state: Finishing, child: ChildHandleNode, proposedUpdate: Any?): Boolean {
 921→        val handle = child.childJob.invokeOnCompletion(
 922→            invokeImmediately = false,
 923→            handler = ChildCompletion(this, state, child, proposedUpdate).asHandler
 924→        )
 925→        if (handle !== NonDisposableHandle) return true // child is not complete and we've started waiting for it
 926→        val nextChild = child.nextChild() ?: return false
 927→        return tryWaitForChild(state, nextChild, proposedUpdate)
 928→    }
 929→
 930→    // ## IMPORTANT INVARIANT: Only one thread can be concurrently invoking this method.
 931→    private fun continueCompleting(state: Finishing, lastChild: ChildHandleNode, proposedUpdate: Any?) {
 932→        assert { this.state === state } // consistency check -- it cannot change while we are waiting for children
 933→        // figure out if we need to wait for next child
 934→        val waitChild = lastChild.nextChild()
 935→        // try wait for next child
 936→        if (waitChild != null && tryWaitForChild(state, waitChild, proposedUpdate)) return // waiting for next child
 937→        // no more children to wait -- try update state
 938→        val finalState = finalizeFinishingState(state, proposedUpdate)
 939→        afterCompletion(finalState)
 940→    }
 941→
 942→    private fun LockFreeLinkedListNode.nextChild(): ChildHandleNode? {
 943→        var cur = this
 944→        while (cur.isRemoved) cur = cur.prevNode // rollback to prev non-removed (or list head)
 945→        while (true) {
 946→            cur = cur.nextNode
 947→            if (cur.isRemoved) continue
 948→            if (cur is ChildHandleNode) return cur
 949→            if (cur is NodeList) return null // checked all -- no more children
 950→        }
 951→    }
 952→
 953→    public final override val children: Sequence<Job> get() = sequence {
 954→        when (val state = this@JobSupport.state) {
 955→            is ChildHandleNode -> yield(state.childJob)
 956→            is Incomplete -> state.list?.let { list ->
 957→                list.forEach<ChildHandleNode> { yield(it.childJob) }
 958→            }
 959→        }
 960→    }
 961→
 962→    @Suppress("OverridingDeprecatedMember")
 963→    public final override fun attachChild(child: ChildJob): ChildHandle {
 964→        /*
 965→         * Note: This function attaches a special ChildHandleNode node object. This node object
 966→         * is handled in a special way on completion on the coroutine (we wait for all of them) and
 967→         * is handled specially by invokeOnCompletion itself -- it adds this node to the list even
 968→         * if the job is already cancelling. For cancelling state child is attached under state lock.
 969→         * It's required to properly wait all children before completion and provide linearizable hierarchy view:
 970→         * If child is attached when the job is already being cancelled, such child will receive immediate notification on
 971→         * cancellation, but parent *will* wait for that child before completion and will handle its exception.
 972→         */
 973→        return invokeOnCompletion(onCancelling = true, handler = ChildHandleNode(child).asHandler) as ChildHandle
 974→    }
 975→
 976→    /**
 977→     * Override to process any exceptions that were encountered while invoking completion handlers
 978→     * installed via [invokeOnCompletion].
 979→     *
 980→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
 981→     */
 982→    internal open fun handleOnCompletionException(exception: Throwable) {
 983→        throw exception
 984→    }
 985→
 986→    /**
 987→     * This function is invoked once as soon as this job is being cancelled for any reason or completes,
 988→     * similarly to [invokeOnCompletion] with `onCancelling` set to `true`.
 989→     *
 990→     * The meaning of [cause] parameter:
 991→     * * Cause is `null` when the job has completed normally.
 992→     * * Cause is an instance of [CancellationException] when the job was cancelled _normally_.
 993→     *   **It should not be treated as an error**. In particular, it should not be reported to error logs.
 994→     * * Otherwise, the job had been cancelled or failed with exception.
 995→     *
 996→     * The specified [cause] is not the final cancellation cause of this job.
 997→     * A job may produce other exceptions while it is failing and the final cause might be different.
 998→     *
 999→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.*
1000→     */
1001→    protected open fun onCancelling(cause: Throwable?) {}
1002→
1003→    /**
1004→     * Returns `true` for scoped coroutines.
1005→     * Scoped coroutine is a coroutine that is executed sequentially within the enclosing scope without any concurrency.
1006→     * Scoped coroutines always handle any exception happened within -- they just rethrow it to the enclosing scope.
1007→     * Examples of scoped coroutines are `coroutineScope`, `withTimeout` and `runBlocking`.
1008→     */
1009→    protected open val isScopedCoroutine: Boolean get() = false
1010→
1011→    /**
1012→     * Returns `true` for jobs that handle their exceptions or integrate them into the job's result via [onCompletionInternal].
1013→     * A valid implementation of this getter should recursively check parent as well before returning `false`.
1014→     *
1015→     * The only instance of the [Job] that does not handle its exceptions is [JobImpl] and its subclass [SupervisorJobImpl].
1016→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.*
1017→     */
1018→    internal open val handlesException: Boolean get() = true
1019→
1020→    /**
1021→     * Handles the final job [exception] that was not handled by the parent coroutine.
1022→     * Returns `true` if it handles exception (so handling at later stages is not needed).
1023→     * It is designed to be overridden by launch-like coroutines
1024→     * (`StandaloneCoroutine` and `ActorCoroutine`) that don't have a result type
1025→     * that can represent exceptions.
1026→     *
1027→     * This method is invoked **exactly once** when the final exception of the job is determined
1028→     * and before it becomes complete. At the moment of invocation the job and all its children are complete.
1029→     */
1030→    protected open fun handleJobException(exception: Throwable): Boolean = false
1031→
1032→    /**
1033→     * Override for completion actions that need to update some external object depending on job's state,
1034→     * right before all the waiters for coroutine's completion are notified.
1035→     *
1036→     * @param state the final state.
1037→     *
1038→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
1039→     */
1040→    protected open fun onCompletionInternal(state: Any?) {}
1041→
1042→    /**
1043→     * Override for the very last action on job's completion to resume the rest of the code in
1044→     * scoped coroutines. It is called when this job is externally completed in an unknown
1045→     * context and thus should resume with a default mode.
1046→     *
1047→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
1048→     */
1049→    protected open fun afterCompletion(state: Any?) {}

1051→    // for nicer debugging
1052→    public override fun toString(): String =
1053→        "${toDebugString()}@$hexAddress"
1054→
1055→    @InternalCoroutinesApi
1056→    public fun toDebugString(): String = "${nameString()}{${stateString(state)}}"
1057→
1058→    /**
1059→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
1060→     */
1061→    internal open fun nameString(): String = classSimpleName
1062→
1063→    private fun stateString(state: Any?): String = when (state) {
1064→        is Finishing -> when {
1065→            state.isCancelling -> "Cancelling"
1066→            state.isCompleting -> "Completing"
1067→            else -> "Active"
1068→        }
1069→        is Incomplete -> if (state.isActive) "Active" else "New"
1070→        is CompletedExceptionally -> "Cancelled"
1071→        else -> "Completed"
1072→    }
1073→
1074→    // Completing & Cancelling states,
1075→    // All updates are guarded by synchronized(this), reads are volatile
1076→    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
1077→    private class Finishing(
1078→        override val list: NodeList,
1079→        isCompleting: Boolean,
1080→        rootCause: Throwable?
1081→    ) : SynchronizedObject(), Incomplete {
1082→        private val _isCompleting = atomic(isCompleting)
1083→        var isCompleting: Boolean
1084→            get() = _isCompleting.value
1085→            set(value) { _isCompleting.value = value }
1086→
1087→        private val _rootCause = atomic(rootCause)
1088→        var rootCause: Throwable? // NOTE: rootCause is kept even when SEALED
1089→            get() = _rootCause.value
1090→            set(value) { _rootCause.value = value }
1091→
1092→        private val _exceptionsHolder = atomic<Any?>(null)
1093→        private var exceptionsHolder: Any? // Contains null | Throwable | ArrayList | SEALED
1094→            get() = _exceptionsHolder.value
1095→            set(value) { _exceptionsHolder.value = value }
1096→
1097→        // Note: cannot be modified when sealed
1098→        val isSealed: Boolean get() = exceptionsHolder === SEALED
1099→        val isCancelling: Boolean get() = rootCause != null
1100→        override val isActive: Boolean get() = rootCause == null // !isCancelling
1101→
1102→        // Seals current state and returns list of exceptions
1103→        // guarded by `synchronized(this)`
1104→        fun sealLocked(proposedException: Throwable?): List<Throwable> {
1105→            val list = when(val eh = exceptionsHolder) { // volatile read
1106→                null -> allocateList()
1107→                is Throwable -> allocateList().also { it.add(eh) }
1108→                is ArrayList<*> -> eh as ArrayList<Throwable>
1109→                else -> error("State is $eh") // already sealed -- cannot happen
1110→            }
1111→            val rootCause = this.rootCause // volatile read
1112→            rootCause?.let { list.add(0, it) } // note -- rootCause goes to the beginning
1113→            if (proposedException != null && proposedException != rootCause) list.add(proposedException)
1114→            exceptionsHolder = SEALED
1115→            return list
1116→        }
1117→
1118→        // guarded by `synchronized(this)`
1119→        fun addExceptionLocked(exception: Throwable) {
1120→            val rootCause = this.rootCause // volatile read
1121→            if (rootCause == null) {
1122→                this.rootCause = exception
1123→                return
1124→            }
1125→            if (exception === rootCause) return // nothing to do
1126→            when (val eh = exceptionsHolder) { // volatile read
1127→                null -> exceptionsHolder = exception
1128→                is Throwable -> {
1129→                    if (exception === eh) return // nothing to do
1130→                    exceptionsHolder = allocateList().apply {
1131→                        add(eh)
1132→                        add(exception)
1133→
1134→                    }
1135→                }
1136→                is ArrayList<*> -> (eh as ArrayList<Throwable>).add(exception)
1137→                else -> error("State is $eh") // already sealed -- cannot happen
1138→            }
1139→        }
1140→
1141→        private fun allocateList() = ArrayList<Throwable>(4)
1142→
1143→        override fun toString(): String =
1144→            "Finishing[cancelling=$isCancelling, completing=$isCompleting, rootCause=$rootCause, exceptions=$exceptionsHolder, list=$list]"
1145→    }
1146→
1147→    private val Incomplete.isCancelling: Boolean
1148→        get() = this is Finishing && isCancelling
1149→
1150→    // Used by parent that is waiting for child completion
1151→    private class ChildCompletion(
1152→        private val parent: JobSupport,
1153→        private val state: Finishing,
1154→        private val child: ChildHandleNode,
1155→        private val proposedUpdate: Any?
1156→    ) : JobNode() {
1157→        override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) {
1158→            parent.continueCompleting(state, child, proposedUpdate)
1159→        }
1160→    }
1161→
1162→    private class AwaitContinuation<T>(
1163→        delegate: Continuation<T>,
1164→        private val job: JobSupport
1165→    ) : CancellableContinuationImpl<T>(delegate, MODE_CANCELLABLE) {
1166→        override fun getContinuationCancellationCause(parent: Job): Throwable {
1167→            val state = job.state
1168→            /*
1169→             * When the job we are waiting for had already completely completed exceptionally or
1170→             * is failing, we shall use its root/completion cause for await's result.
1171→             */
1172→            if (state is Finishing) state.rootCause?.let { return it }
1173→            if (state is CompletedExceptionally) return state.cause
1174→            return parent.getCancellationException()
1175→        }
1176→
1177→        protected override fun nameString(): String =
1178→            "AwaitContinuation"
1179→    }
1180→
1181→    /*
1182→     * =================================================================================================
1183→     * This is ready-to-use implementation for Deferred interface.
1184→     * However, it is not type-safe. Conceptually it just exposes the value of the underlying
1185→     * completed state as `Any?`
1186→     * =================================================================================================
1187→     */
1188→
1189→    public val isCompletedExceptionally: Boolean get() = state is CompletedExceptionally
1190→
1191→    public fun getCompletionExceptionOrNull(): Throwable? {
1192→        val state = this.state
1193→        check(state !is Incomplete) { "This job has not completed yet" }
1194→        return state.exceptionOrNull
1195→    }
1196→
1197→    /**
1198→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
1199→     */
1200→    internal fun getCompletedInternal(): Any? {
1201→        val state = this.state
1202→        check(state !is Incomplete) { "This job has not completed yet" }
1203→        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) throw state.cause
1204→        return state.unboxState()
1205→    }
1206→
1207→    /**
1208→     * @suppress **This is unstable API and it is subject to change.**
1209→     */
1210→    protected suspend fun awaitInternal(): Any? {
1211→        // fast-path -- check state (avoid extra object creation)
1212→        while (true) { // lock-free loop on state
1213→            val state = this.state
1214→            if (state !is Incomplete) {
1215→                // already complete -- just return result
1216→                if (state is CompletedExceptionally) { // Slow path to recover stacktrace
1217→                    recoverAndThrow(state.cause)
1218→                }
1219→                return state.unboxState()
1220→
1221→            }
1222→            if (startInternal(state) >= 0) break // break unless needs to retry
1223→        }
1224→        return awaitSuspend() // slow-path
1225→    }
1226→
1227→    private suspend fun awaitSuspend(): Any? = suspendCoroutineUninterceptedOrReturn { uCont ->
1228→        /*
1229→         * Custom code here, so that parent coroutine that is using await
1230→         * on its child deferred (async) coroutine would throw the exception that this child had
1231→         * thrown and not a JobCancellationException.
1232→         */
1233→        val cont = AwaitContinuation(uCont.intercepted(), this)
1234→        // we are mimicking suspendCancellableCoroutine here and call initCancellability, too.
1235→        cont.initCancellability()
1236→        cont.disposeOnCancellation(invokeOnCompletion(ResumeAwaitOnCompletion(cont).asHandler))
1237→        cont.getResult()
1238→    }
1239→
1240→    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
1241→    protected val onAwaitInternal: SelectClause1<*> get() = SelectClause1Impl<Any?>(
1242→        clauseObject = this@JobSupport,
1243→        regFunc = JobSupport::onAwaitInternalRegFunc as RegistrationFunction,
1244→        processResFunc = JobSupport::onAwaitInternalProcessResFunc as ProcessResultFunction
1245→    )
1246→
1247→    @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")
1248→    private fun onAwaitInternalRegFunc(select: SelectInstance<*>, ignoredParam: Any?) {
1249→        while (true) {
1250→            val state = this.state
1251→            if (state !is Incomplete) {
1252→                val result = if (state is CompletedExceptionally) state else state.unboxState()
1253→                select.selectInRegistrationPhase(result)
1254→                return
1255→            }
1256→            if (startInternal(state) >= 0) break // break unless needs to retry
1257→        }
1258→        val disposableHandle = invokeOnCompletion(SelectOnAwaitCompletionHandler(select).asHandler)
1259→        select.disposeOnCompletion(disposableHandle)
1260→    }
1261→
1262→    @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")
1263→    private fun onAwaitInternalProcessResFunc(ignoredParam: Any?, result: Any?): Any? {
1264→        if (result is CompletedExceptionally) throw result.cause
1265→        return result
1266→    }
1267→
1268→    private inner class SelectOnAwaitCompletionHandler(
1269→        private val select: SelectInstance<*>
1270→    ) : JobNode() {
1271→        override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) {
1272→            val state = this@JobSupport.state
1273→            val result = if (state is CompletedExceptionally) state else state.unboxState()
1274→            select.trySelect(this@JobSupport, result)
1275→        }
1276→    }
1277→}

1283→internal fun Any?.boxIncomplete(): Any? = if (this is Incomplete) IncompleteStateBox(this) else this
1284→internal fun Any?.unboxState(): Any? = (this as? IncompleteStateBox)?.state ?: this

1307→@PublishedApi // for a custom job in the test module
1308→internal open class JobImpl(parent: Job?) : JobSupport(true), CompletableJob {
1309→    init { initParentJob(parent) }
1310→    override val onCancelComplete get() = true
1311→    /*
1312→     * Check whether parent is able to handle exceptions as well.
1313→     * With this check, an exception in that pattern will be handled once:
1314→     * ```
1315→     * launch {
1316→     *     val child = Job(coroutineContext[Job])
1317→     *     launch(child) { throw ... }
1318→     * }
1319→     * ```
1320→     */
1321→    override val handlesException: Boolean = handlesException()
1322→    override fun complete() = makeCompleting(Unit)
1323→    override fun completeExceptionally(exception: Throwable): Boolean =
1324→        makeCompleting(CompletedExceptionally(exception))
1325→
1326→    @JsName("handlesExceptionF")
1327→    private fun handlesException(): Boolean {
1328→        var parentJob = (parentHandle as? ChildHandleNode)?.job ?: return false
1329→        while (true) {
1330→            if (parentJob.handlesException) return true
1331→            parentJob = (parentJob.parentHandle as? ChildHandleNode)?.job ?: return false
1332→        }
1333→    }
1334→}

1381→private class InvokeOnCompletion(
1382→    private val handler: CompletionHandler
1383→) : JobNode()  {

1385→}

1387→private class ResumeOnCompletion(
1388→    private val continuation: Continuation<Unit>
1389→) : JobNode() {

1391→}
1392→
1393→private class ResumeAwaitOnCompletion<T>(
1394→    private val continuation: CancellableContinuationImpl<T>
1395→) : JobNode() {
1396→    override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) {
1397→        val state = job.state
1398→        assert { state !is Incomplete }
1399→        if (state is CompletedExceptionally) {
1400→            // Resume with with the corresponding exception to preserve it
1401→            continuation.resumeWithException(state.cause)
1402→        } else {
1403→            // Resuming with value in a cancellable way (AwaitContinuation is configured for this mode).
1404→            @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
1405→            continuation.resume(state.unboxState() as T)
1406→        }
1407→    }
1408→}

1411→    private val handle: DisposableHandle

1416→// -------- invokeOnCancellation nodes
1417→
1418→/**
1419→ * Marker for node that shall be invoked on in _cancelling_ state.
1420→ * **Note: may be invoked multiple times.**
1421→ */
1422→internal abstract class JobCancellingNode : JobNode()
1423→
1424→private class InvokeOnCancelling(
1425→    private val handler: CompletionHandler
1426→) : JobCancellingNode()  {
1427→    // delegate handler shall be invoked at most once, so here is an additional flag
1428→    private val _invoked = atomic(0) // todo: replace with atomic boolean after migration to recent atomicFu
1429→    override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) {
1430→        if (_invoked.compareAndSet(0, 1)) handler.invoke(cause)
1431→    }
1432→}
1433→
1434→internal class ChildHandleNode(
1435→    @JvmField val childJob: ChildJob
1436→) : JobCancellingNode(), ChildHandle {
1437→    override val parent: Job get() = job
1438→    override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) = childJob.parentCancelled(job)
1439→    override fun childCancelled(cause: Throwable): Boolean = job.childCancelled(cause)
1440→}
1441→
1442→// Same as ChildHandleNode, but for cancellable continuation
1443→internal class ChildContinuation(
1444→    @JvmField val child: CancellableContinuationImpl<*>
1445→) : JobCancellingNode() {
1446→    override fun invoke(cause: Throwable?) {
1447→        child.parentCancelled(child.getContinuationCancellationCause(job))
1448→    }
1449→}
1450→
1451→

conversation_history.content:
1→/*
  2→ * Copyright 2016-2021 JetBrains s.r.o. Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 license.
  3→ */
  4→@file:Suppress("DEPRECATION_ERROR")
  5→
  6→package kotlinx.coroutines
  7→
  8→import kotlinx.coroutines.CoroutineStart.*
  9→import kotlinx.coroutines.intrinsics.*
 10→import kotlin.coroutines.*
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Abstract base class for implementation of coroutines in coroutine builders.
 14→ *
 15→ * This class implements completion [Continuation], [Job], and [CoroutineScope] interfaces.
 16→ * It stores the result of continuation in the state of the job.
 17→ * This coroutine waits for children coroutines to finish before completing and
 18→ * fails through an intermediate _failing_ state.
 19→ *
 20→ * The following methods are available for override:
 21→ *
 22→ * * [onStart] is invoked when the coroutine was created in non-active state and is being [started][Job.start].
 23→ * * [onCancelling] is invoked as soon as the coroutine starts being cancelled for any reason (or completes).
 24→ * * [onCompleted] is invoked when the coroutine completes with a value.
 25→ * * [onCancelled] in invoked when the coroutine completes with an exception (cancelled).
 26→ *
 27→ * @param parentContext the context of the parent coroutine.
 28→ * @param initParentJob specifies whether the parent-child relationship should be instantiated directly
 29→ *               in `AbstractCoroutine` constructor. If set to `false`, it's the responsibility of the child class
 30→ *               to invoke [initParentJob] manually.
 31→ * @param active when `true` (by default), the coroutine is created in the _active_ state, otherwise it is created in the _new_ state.
 32→ *               See [Job] for details.
 33→ *
 34→ * @suppress **This an internal API and should not be used from general code.**
 35→ */
 36→@InternalCoroutinesApi
 37→public abstract class AbstractCoroutine<in T>(
 38→    parentContext: CoroutineContext,
 39→    initParentJob: Boolean,
 40→    active: Boolean
 41→) : JobSupport(active), Job, Continuation<T>, CoroutineScope {
 42→
 43→    init {
 44→        /*
 45→         * Setup parent-child relationship between the parent in the context and the current coroutine.
 46→         * It may cause this coroutine to become _cancelling_ if the parent is already cancelled.
 47→         * It is dangerous to install parent-child relationship here if the coroutine class
 48→         * operates its state from within onCancelled or onCancelling
 49→         * (with exceptions for rx integrations that can't have any parent)
 50→         */
 51→        if (initParentJob) initParentJob(parentContext[Job])
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    /**
 55→     * The context of this coroutine that includes this coroutine as a [Job].
 56→     */
 57→    @Suppress("LeakingThis")
 58→    public final override val context: CoroutineContext = parentContext + this
 59→
 60→    /**
 61→     * The context of this scope which is the same as the [context] of this coroutine.
 62→     */
 63→    public override val coroutineContext: CoroutineContext get() = context
 64→
 65→    override val isActive: Boolean get() = super.isActive
 66→
 67→    /**
 68→     * This function is invoked once when the job was completed normally with the specified [value],
 69→     * right before all the waiters for the coroutine's completion are notified.
 70→     */
 71→    protected open fun onCompleted(value: T) {}
 72→
 73→    /**
 74→     * This function is invoked once when the job was cancelled with the specified [cause],
 75→     * right before all the waiters for coroutine's completion are notified.
 76→     *
 77→     * **Note:** the state of the coroutine might not be final yet in this function and should not be queried.
 78→     * You can use [completionCause] and [completionCauseHandled] to recover parameters that we passed
 79→     * to this `onCancelled` invocation only when [isCompleted] returns `true`.
 80→     *
 81→     * @param cause The cancellation (failure) cause
 82→     * @param handled `true` if the exception was handled by parent (always `true` when it is a [CancellationException])
 83→     */
 84→    protected open fun onCancelled(cause: Throwable, handled: Boolean) {}
 85→
 86→    override fun cancellationExceptionMessage(): String = "$classSimpleName was cancelled"
 87→
 88→    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
 89→    protected final override fun onCompletionInternal(state: Any?) {
 90→        if (state is CompletedExceptionally)
 91→            onCancelled(state.cause, state.handled)
 92→        else
 93→            onCompleted(state as T)
 94→    }
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Completes execution of this with coroutine with the specified result.
 98→     */
 99→    public final override fun resumeWith(result: Result<T>) {
100→        val state = makeCompletingOnce(result.toState())
101→        if (state === COMPLETING_WAITING_CHILDREN) return
102→        afterResume(state)
103→    }
104→
105→    protected open fun afterResume(state: Any?): Unit = afterCompletion(state)
106→
107→    internal final override fun handleOnCompletionException(exception: Throwable) {
108→        handleCoroutineException(context, exception)
109→    }
110→
111→    internal override fun nameString(): String {
112→        val coroutineName = context.coroutineName ?: return super.nameString()
113→        return "\"$coroutineName\":${super.nameString()}"
114→    }
115→
116→    /**
117→     * Starts this coroutine with the given code [block] and [start] strategy.
118→     * This function shall be invoked at most once on this coroutine.
119→     * 
120→     * * [DEFAULT] uses [startCoroutineCancellable].
121→     * * [ATOMIC] uses [startCoroutine].
122→     * * [UNDISPATCHED] uses [startCoroutineUndispatched].
123→     * * [LAZY] does nothing.
124→     */
125→    public fun <R> start(start: CoroutineStart, receiver: R, block: suspend R.() -> T) {
126→        start(block, receiver, this)
127→    }
128→}
129→
